title: The Macro Minute: How is the US economy performing?

Happy Tuesday out there team 42. It's
your skipper here at Darius Dell to
present our macro minute for Tuesday,
November 25th, 2025. As always, we'll
start with the executive summary from
today's lead off morning note. So, let's
hop right in. Today's key macro question
is how was the US economy performing?
The short answer is not great. The U and
our U-shaped economy thesis is deepening
and our nearly four-year-old sticky
inflation theme was incrementally
supported by the data. terms of the
analytical nuance, the weak positive
impulses in headline core, core x-ray
services and services PPI and the strong
positive impulse in goods PPI each
supported our tariff pig in the
inflation python thesis. Additionally,
the increase in the conference board's
one-year median CPI metric to a
three-month high of 4.8 supported our
staky inflation theme. These hawkish
developments should be juxtaposed with
dovish retail sales data which generally
supported our U-shaped economy thesis
via the weak positive impulse and
nominal headline retail sales, the weak
negative impulse in core retail sales,
and the strong negative impulse in auto
sales, which are down 27% on a
three-month annualized basis, the lowest
since June. The November Conference
Board Consumer Confidence survey data
also supported our U-shaped economy
thesis. The Fed has a clear window to
cut and supply the liquidity the market
needs as it is ever going to have.
Considering that our secular inflation
model has been signaling a 3%
equilibrium core PC inflation rate since
January of 2022, the fact that they
aren't doing so suggests the institution
is back to overweing the risks to
achieving its arbitrary 2% inflation
target, risking further contraction in
the labor market and a funding crisis in
the repo market. This policy gaff may
trigger a meaningful correction or crash
in risk assets akin to Q4 2018.
All told, the latest key highfrequency
economic data confirm the US economy
remains U-shaped in aggregate terms and
K-shaped in distributional terms. The
Fed will exacerbate these dynamics by
falling further behind the rate and
liquidity curves to defend its arbitrary
2% inflation target, remind the Trump
administration who's actually in
control, or both. POTUS' bullying of POW
may have unintended consequences. As
always, members of our global investor
community can trust that our
institutional grade risk management
overlays, KISS and Dr. Mo, will help our
portfolios navigate these emergent risks
better than investors attempting to
manage risk without systematic rules or
relying on fundamental research alone.
Transitioning to our for each micro
dashboard. As always, we'll wrap up with
a question from our community. This
one's titled K-shaped economy. It reads,
"I thought I'd share this article from
Michael Green. It's a must-read. The
article does a great job of explaining
why so many feel they're not doing well
uh in economic um economically. Bottom
of the K. Best I've seen. Uh says uh so
uh yeah, I got I gave out so shout out
to our friend Mike Green over at
Simplify Asset Management for putting
together this really thoughtful data
driven piece. I highly recommend
everyone gives it a checkout. Uh it's I
think it's titled My Life is a Lie part
one. I think it's going to be a second
part or second or third part to the
series, but definitely check that out. I
would um you know, respectfully
disagree, however, that this is the best
we've seen. I don't know that this
person has reviewed our macro scouting
reports that explain all the uh core
dynamics that are contributing to
causing and ultimately resulting from
the K-shaped economy and ultimately how
that is impacting both the political
cycles and the market cycles as a
function of the political cycle. So,
this is something we've obviously done a
tremendous amount of research on.
spanning dozens and dozens of slides
across many months. Uh so uh the most
recent um most recent uh depiction we've
we've communicated was in our most
recent around the war from last week. Uh
if you go to my Twitter uh today uh just
look at uh uh our post. Here's
everything we need to know to solve the
K-shaped economy crisis. The KEC uh
that's a post that we put out today is a
clip from our uh our November 20th round
the horn presentation. uh it's also on a
pin post or feature post rather on my uh
LinkedIn profile as well. So uh if
you're a policy maker, someone in
leadership uh you know whether it be in
your community, your church, your your
company, uh if you're in the media,
please make time to take take the
however long this takes. Is it seven,
eight, nine minutes? 9 minutes and 39
seconds. Can you please if you're in a
leadership position, particularly if
you're in the media and in politics,
take the nine minutes it takes to review
that analysis that I have not seen
anyone produce that detailed of analysis
in terms of the causal driver of this
K-shaped US economy. Uh, and ultimately
we all need to get on the same page to
fix the K-shaped economy. This is not an
appropriate outcome for an advanced
society that's as wealthy as we are. And
I'll tell you right now, if we continue
down this path, based on the analysis
we've read from the fourth turning,
based on the analysis we've observed in
Peterin's books, based on the analysis
we've observed, all observed in Ray
Alio's books, I'm telling you right now,
this is going to end very poorly for
this American thing we call this is
experiment experiment that we call the
American society. And so if you don't
think that's going to have economic and
financial market consequences, then
you're burying your head in the sand and
not paying attention to the big drivers
and the big things that actually matter
in a fork turning in this type of um in
this type of age. So again, highly
recommend you guys go out there and
check that out. Uh you know, we got to
find solutions. You know, as someone who
grew up at the very bottom of the
K-shaped economy, I've been homeless
six, seven times in my life, probably
seven if I do the math. You know, I
can't tell you how many times I've I've
gotten food from a food bank before in
my life. As someone who actually
understands what the damn affordability
crisis means, unlike the rest of the
people just tweeting about it on Wall
Street and talking about it on TV, I'm
one of the very few people who actually
can do the analysis that actually
understands how these people feel and
the stress that they're under and the
negative health consequences that is
coming from all that stress.
So trust me when I say that there's not
a person on Wall Street that cares about
ending the K-shaped economy crisis more
than Darius Dell because this is an
unacceptable outcome for the people
gasping for economic oxygen at the
bottom of the case. So join me in trying
to fix this. We'll wrap it up there.
Darius Dell here presenting our macro
minute for Tuesday, November 25th, 2025.
Best of luck out there today. We'll
catch you back here tomorrow. Cheers.
